article.title: How safe is BDSM? A literature review on fatal outcome in BDSM play

authorized_source.normalization: The authorized XML ended with a closing angle bracket and no newline. One terminal LF was added for repository text normalization; both remote and local digests are recorded.

excluded_routes: United States PMC article and PDF endpoints
Europe PMC generated PDF and supplementary routes

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.source_license_scope: The JATS states that article reuse, sharing, adaptation, distribution, and reproduction are permitted with attribution, license link, and change notice, subject to separately credited third-party material.
license.third_party_component_policy: No figure, source table layout, or externally credited expressive material is emitted in the training Markdown.

review_method.automation_boundary: XML parsing was used to navigate metadata, sections, tables, and aggregate counts. The training Markdown was manually selected, rewritten, checked against the JATS, and critically scoped. Raw extraction was not emitted as training prose.
reviewed_regions: all four source tables at disposition level
references and associated data at disposition level

source_internal_issues: The 64.3 percent substance-involvement claim does not explicitly name its denominator; it appears to be 9 of 14 cases with some participant toxicology or intoxication information.
The source Table 1 descriptions labeled traumatic and positional asphyxia appear inverted.
The results describe two cases with breath-control instruction and CPR teaching, while the safeguards discussion describes three cases with instruction and CPR discussion.
A fatal-only case compilation with no nonfatal comparison group cannot estimate correlation between substances or knowledge and fatal outcome.
Publication-derived cases and one institutional autopsy series cannot establish population prevalence, incidence, or per-scene risk.

training_boundary.no_technique_transfer: The corpus contains no breath-control, neck-compression, bondage, suspension, or release configuration instructions.
training_boundary.no_prevalence_transfer: All 17-case findings remain publication-derived and cannot become general BDSM prevalence or incidence claims.

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign PMCID PMC8813685 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
